etymology.description: semantic 人(人) (rén, "person") + phonetic 今(今) (jīn) -> dark, concealed
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 人(人) (rén, "person") suggests the presence or activity of a person, while the phonetic component 今(今) (jīn) provides the sound. The combined imagery suggests something hidden or concealed, akin to a person being in a dark or secretive state.
